intents.prompt: I want to fit a linear regression but I don't think the right library is even pulled in yet. What should I add to my build-depends to get that?
Nothing in my current setup can parse JSON. Which Hackage package should I install for that, and what's the dependency line I drop into the cell?
I've got dataframe installed already — what's the call for dropping rows that have missing values, and which module is it in?
Show me everything Granite.Svg exports so I can see what render helpers are available.
I need something already on hand that turns a [Double] into a single Double — a summary statistic basically. I don't know its name, just the shape [Double] -> Double.
There must be an installed function with signature roughly _ :: Ord a => [a] -> [a] for sorting — can you find it by that type?
Before I write the next cell, remind me what values and functions I've already defined in this session and their types so I don't recompute them.
What's currently in scope from my earlier cells? Give me the live variables and their types.
Without actually running it, what's the type of `map fst . filter snd`?
I already know about the Traversable class — just tell me its definition and kind, don't run anything.
Have you got a ready-made cell I can paste and tweak that reads a CSV into a dataframe? I want the whole snippet, not docs.
Give me a worked example cell showing the typed-column idiom for dataframe so I can adapt it.
Before I load /data/sales.csv into a dataframe, I want to see the delimiter, whether the first row is headers, and the guessed column types.
Sniff the first few rows of ./measurements.tsv for me so I can figure out the schema before reading it.
Give me the lay of the land for this notebook — each cell's id, type, language, and its first line.
I just opened this notebook; what cells are in it and which ones are flagged as broken?
Pull up cell 7 in full — I need both the code I wrote and whatever it produced or errored with.
Let me see the complete source plus output of the cell with id 3.
I don't need the code for cell 12, just tell me what it printed and whether it errored.
What did cell 5 actually return? Skip the source, only the result.
Which cells mention the string `readCsv` anywhere in their source? Show me the matches with a bit of context.
Find every cell whose code contains `groupBy` so I can see all the places it's typed out.
Hand me a single snapshot of the entire notebook — every cell's id, position, type, language, and source — in one shot rather than reading them one by one.
Dump all the cell sources at once so I can review the whole thing together.
Add a new cell at the bottom that imports Data.List and prints `sort [3,1,2]`, and run it.
Tack a fresh markdown cell onto the end of the notebook with a heading for the analysis section.
Cell 9 is one I added earlier and it's throwing an error — rewrite its whole body to use `fromMaybe 0` instead and re-run it.
Swap out the entire contents of cell 4 (which I created) for a corrected version with the import fixed, and run it again.
There's a duplicate cell, id 11, that I can't salvage — just get rid of it.
Remove cell 6 entirely; it's a dead end and it's blocking me from appending new cells.
Cell 2 is one the user wrote, so don't just apply it — suggest a revision that handles the empty-list case and let them approve it.
For the user-authored cell 8, draft a change that adds a type signature but hold it for their review instead of running it.
Just re-run cell 5 as-is and tell me what it gives — I haven't changed anything in it.
Run the existing cell 10 again so it picks up the new binding from upstream.
I want to quickly try `replicate 3 'x'` somewhere throwaway that won't touch my notebook or its variables.
Test this self-contained snippet in a sandbox that can't see any of my cells: `let xs = [1..10] in sum xs`.
Is the kernel actually busy working or has it wedged? Just tell me its state right now without making me wait.
Quick non-blocking check — is the runtime cold, idle, executing, or building at the moment?
I just kicked off a long cell — hang on until it finishes and then tell me the kernel's state.
Wait for the currently running cell to settle before we do anything else.
This cell has been spinning forever — try to abort whatever it's currently running, but don't nuke the whole kernel yet.
Stop the in-progress computation; I want to cancel the cell that's executing right now.
Interrupt didn't free it — the process is truly stuck. Force-kill the kernel and bring it back up clean, but don't rebuild packages or re-run my cells.
The runtime is wedged and won't respond. Hard-reset it: respawn a fresh process reusing the installed packages.
Show me the curated signature card for Granite.Spec — and I haven't run any cell yet, so it can't rely on a live session.
Give me the reference card for the DataFrame.Functions module from the cheat sheet, no running session needed.
I know there's a function called `pivot` — explain in prose what it actually does, beyond just its type.
Walk me through the documentation for `interpolate` — I want to understand its behaviour, not just the signature.
I'm about to use `columnAsList` but I'm not sure which module it comes from — give me the exact import line to paste.
What's the import for the operator `(.|.)`? I know the name, just need the module it lives in.
I have a FilePath and I want to end up with an IO DataFrame — find me a function whose signature is `FilePath -> IO DataFrame`.
Hoogle-style: I need something shaped like `Double -> Picture`. What matches that arrow signature?
I've got the signature for `groupBy` but I keep getting the argument order wrong — show me one real line that actually calls it.
Give me a concrete invocation of `lineGraph` so I can see how its arguments fit together before I write mine.
Show me everything that's currently broken across the notebook in one go — the failing cell ids plus their actual error messages, not just a broken/not-broken flag.
Roll up all the cells that are erroring right now with their messages so I don't have to open each one.
Where does `trainModel` get defined in this notebook, and which cells use it? I want the canonical defining cell, not every substring hit.
Is `df` actually in scope anywhere, and which cell originally binds it?
Before I delete cell 4, what would break — which downstream cells depend on it and would re-run or fail?
For cell 9, show me just the upstream side — which cells feed it the names it consumes.
